text: Does a sech^2 soliton (v amp=2.0 at x=-5) in coupled Burgers-swept-KdV survive to T=8 with perturbation u0 = 0.5*v0^2 + 0.2*v0 from the m=0 Gardner reduction, preserving a dominant peak with amplitude>=1.0, mass(v) drift<8%, bounded |u|,|v|<15?

method: Fourier pseudospectral spatial derivatives (no dealiasing, no filter) + explicit classical RK4 over full RHS (both u-eqn and v-eqn incl. v_xxx)
bank_use_rationale: Per progressive-complexity discipline E1 must be the simplest baseline. Bank confirms Fourier pseudospectral is the appropriate spatial baseline for KdV-class (kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass) and BKdV-S1 round-2 used the same Nx=256, L=30 grid with explicit RK4 successfully. I deliberately omit dealiasing to observe whether the baseline alone suffices — if aliasing manifests (BKdV-S1 indicates it can), E2 adds 2/3 dealiasing as the single-component upgrade. dt=1e-4 sits below BKdV-S1 RK4 CFL estimate ~1.5e-4 (no-dealias regime).

rationale: Classic aliasing blow-up: with NO dealiasing, the quadratic products (v^2, u*v, v*v_x, u*u_x) fold high-k content back into low-k modes and the dispersive v_xxx term amplifies these spurious modes. sup_u blows up to 2e6 by t=0.5 while sup_v has already broken the 15 bound (137). Mass(v) is still conserved up to that point only because the snapshot is still at t=0. This is exactly the failure mode BKdV-S1 r2 attributes to aliasing (not v_xxx stiffness) when 2/3 cutoff is absent.

Add 2/3-rule dealiasing as a SINGLE-COMPONENT upgrade over E1. BKdV-S1 r2 demonstrates that for an essentially identical grid (Nx=256, L=30) and the same explicit RK4 time integrator, adding 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product cures the aliasing blow-up and reaches T=10 with bounded sup_v and machine-zero mass drift. We retain the same time integrator (RK4) and the same dt — one change only.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing (zero |k_idx|>Nx/3) applied to all nonlinear products (v^2, u*v, u*u_x, v*v_x) AND the dealiasing mask is also applied to v_xxx so any aliased high-k feedback is suppressed; explicit classical RK4 over full RHS. Single-component change over E1: only added dealiasing mask.
bank_use_rationale: Positive: BKdV-S1 (round-2 deep-synthesis depth=1 entry) confirms 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product in coupled BKdV with Nx=256, L=30, dt=2e-4, RK4 reaches T=10 cleanly; my v amp=2.0 is below BKdV-S1's stress amp=3 so headroom is comfortable. kb-gardner-G2 and kb-gardner-KdV-method-transfer corroborate that dealiased spectral is the canonical method for soliton-stability problems in this regime — they also warn 'KdV ICs at the same amplitude produce spurious radiation', but my task explicitly uses the sech^2 (KdV-like) IC and tolerates radiation as long as a dominant peak survives, so this warning is informational, not blocking. Rejected: kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass — switching to finite-volume MUSCL/Godunov for the u-eqn would be a multi-component swap (FV + Riemann solver replaces spectral) and would lose the spectral v_xxx accuracy critical to soliton phase; only single-component changes allowed at E2.

rationale: Dealiasing perfectly suppresses aliasing (tail above 2/3-band is machine zero for both fields). v field is well-behaved: mass conserved to machine precision, peak amplitude stays >=0.87 throughout and recovers to 1.45 at T=8 (well above the 1.0 phenomenon threshold; |v|<2 throughout, well under 15). v shows a dominant single positive peak at all snapshots. BUT the u-field grows from sup_u=2.4 initially to 20.8 at T=8 — VIOLATING the |u|<15 phenomenon bound. Diagnostic shows top-decile-of-resolved-band energy in u reaches 6-10% — i.e., the u-eqn (3 u u_x with no dispersion/dissipation) is undergoing Burgers-shock steepening at the band edge. This is a different failure mode from F1 (which was aliasing); now we have a physical-PDE steepening problem at the trailing edge of the u-eqn solution space.

Single-component upgrade: add a small high-k spectral damping (low-amplitude hyperviscosity, -nu * k^8) applied ONLY to the u-equation, calibrated to engage on the top decile of the 2/3-resolved band (k near k_cut=Nx/3) without affecting the soliton-bearing modes of v. This is the minimal change that targets the diagnosed failure (high-k pile-up in u from inviscid Burgers steepening) while preserving spectral accuracy on v (and especially v_xxx) and keeping the same RK4 time integrator and same dt and same dealias mask. We do NOT switch to MUSCL+Godunov for u (kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass) because that would be a multi-component swap (FV + Riemann solver replaces spectral + add operator splitting); progressive-complexity discipline allows only one new component at E3.

method: Same as E2 (Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing + classical explicit RK4) PLUS one single new component: spectral hyperviscosity term -nu_h * (k^2)^p applied ONLY to the u-equation with p=4 and nu_h calibrated so damping rate at k_cut is O(1)/T. v-equation discretization untouched (still preserves dispersive accuracy of v_xxx and soliton phase). Single-component change vs E2: added high-k hyperviscosity term on u only.
bank_use_rationale: Positive: kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass, kb-burgers-Godunov-preShock-smooth, and kb-general-firstOrder-Godunov-preShock-baseline all consistently identify the Burgers-shock-steepening failure mode in the u-component of coupled BKdV as the dominant late-time issue and recommend a discretization that introduces local entropy-consistent dissipation on the u-advection (MUSCL+Godunov in particular). I adopt the spirit of their recommendation — bounded numerical dissipation localized to high k where the steepening lives — but implement it as spectral hyperviscosity, which is the SINGLE-component upgrade compatible with the spectral framework (MUSCL+Godunov would be a multi-component swap to FV). Rejected: kb-shallowWater-LaxFriedrichs-stable-smeared and kb-shallowWater-HLL-dam-break-pass are shallow-water-flavored Riemann-solver recommendations for variable-depth hyperbolic systems; not applicable here (no dry zones, our u-field is not a depth variable, and these would require complete restructuring of the u-eqn into FV form).

rationale: All four phenomenon-target conditions satisfied with margin: max_v(T)=1.013 >= 1.0 threshold (equal to 0.5 * 2.0 IC peak), mass_v drift = 0 (machine-zero, <<8%), sup_u=9.49 < 15, sup_v=1.01 < 15. The single-component hyperviscosity addition on u contained the Burgers steepening that broke E2, while the v-eqn spectral discretization remained fully accurate so the dispersive soliton structure was preserved. Final v(x,T) has a dominant peak at x=11.48 with amplitude 1.013, the 2nd-highest local maximum is 0.83 (ratio 1.22), so the dominant peak is clearly distinguishable from the surrounding radiation pattern (which is the expected Gardner-perturbation radiation from a non-Gardner IC, per kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation).

E3 self-assessed useful — all four phenomenon targets met with margin. Stopping early at end of iter 3 (which is also the last allowed iteration anyway). pred_results/T_A.npy contains the final 9-snapshot tensor.
